title: bus.notify v1
description: Producer-agnostic notification channel — the headline that goes to a phone lock-screen, a discord channel, an ntfy topic, or a peer Claude session. Producers emit; hearth (home-automation) is the canonical consumer and routes to actual transports per `channels`. The bus carries the message, not the delivery — consumers decide which transports they actually wire (MQTT for phone, discord webhook, ntfy POST, session://<peer-id> for CCM). `summary` is hard-capped at 140 chars so it fits on a lock-screen; `body` is the longer detail for transports that support it (discord, ntfy). `correlation_id` is the ULID of the upstream event/cycle/PR so a phone tap can deep-link back; `source_event_type` records the upstream channel name for traceability without bloating the schema. `dedup_key` lets consumers collapse a flood of identical notifies into one. No transport is required — a notify with `channels:['session://eric-pane-3']` is just as valid as one with `channels:['phone','discord']`.

priority.description: Severity. Consumers may filter on this — e.g. only `critical` notifies route to phone, everything else stays in discord/session.

items.description: Transport identifier. `phone`, `discord`, `ntfy` are the named transports. `session://<peer-id>` addresses a specific Claude Code session by its peer id (see sdk/shell/nervous peer).

channels.description: Ordered list of transports the producer requests. Consumers fan out to the ones they have wired; unwired transports are logged-and-dropped (not fatal).

summary.description: Headline that fits on a lock-screen notification. Producers should write this as a complete sentence — no leading project tag, no trailing ellipsis.

source_project.description: Lowercase identifier of the project that emitted this notify (e.g. `hearth-loom`, `nervous-bus`, `autobench`, `deer-flow`). Used by consumers for routing and dedup scoping.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the notify was produced. Mirrors the envelope `time` for consumers reading the flat data payload.

correlation_id.description: Optional ULID of the upstream event/cycle/PR/bead. A phone tap on the notification deep-links back to whatever this id resolves to in the consumer's persistence layer.

body.description: Optional longer detail. Transports that support multi-line bodies (discord embed, ntfy markdown) render this; lock-screen transports show only `summary`.

deep_link.description: Optional URI — GitHub PR URL, dugout URL, deer-flow gateway path, etc. The actionable target for a tap-back from the phone.

source_event_type.description: Optional upstream bus channel name that triggered this notify (e.g. `autobench.cycle.report.v1`, `loom.lifecycle.pr.v1`). For traceability — consumers can correlate notifies with the original event stream.

dedup_key.description: Optional opaque key. Consumers MAY collapse multiple notifies sharing this key into a single delivered notification (e.g. coalesce repeated CI failure pings).
